findings.evidence: `rg -n "eq_ofReal_re|timeSlice_apply_zero" code/TauCeti/Analysis/PositiveDefinite` shows existing `map_zero_eq_ofReal_re` in `Basic.lean:138` and `diagonal_eq_ofReal_re` in `SemigroupGroup.lean:279`, while the new slice file only has `timeSlice_apply_zero_nonneg/im/re_nonneg` at lines 92, 99, and 104.
findings.fix: Add `IsSemigroupGroupPD.timeSlice_apply_zero_eq_ofReal_re` after the real-part lemma, matching `IsPositiveDefinite.map_zero_eq_ofReal_re` and `IsSemigroupGroupPD.diagonal_eq_ofReal_re`.
findings.issue: The fixed-time origin API stops at `timeSlice_apply_zero_nonneg`, `timeSlice_apply_zero_im`, and `timeSlice_apply_zero_re_nonneg`, but does not expose `F (t,0) = ((F (t,0)).re : ℂ)`; downstream normalization/Bochner code otherwise has to rebuild this normal form from `Complex.ext` every time.

summary: The slice API exposes the main kernel and finite-form lemmas, but it leaves out the normal-form lemma for the slice diagonal value that the surrounding positive-definite API consistently provides.
